ResearcherNotesProject: Iraq's FIRST LNG import terminal. Excelerate Energy signed a definitive commercial agreement with a subsidiary of Iraq's Ministry of Electricity on 28 Oct 2025 (5-year regas+supply, extension options; ~$450M). Dedicated FSRU = HD Hyundai Heavy Industries Hull 3407 (170,000 cbm), delivery 2026, COD expected 2026. CAPACITY is a range, recorded as design throughput: min contracted ~250 MMscf/d (~1.9 MTPA / 2.6 bcm/y), guaranteed design ~500 MMscf/d (~3.8 MTPA), vessel max ~1,000 MMscf/d (~7.6 MTPA) — confirm which figure GEM should carry (methodology: record max in spreadsheet, range in wiki Background). STATUS construction vs proposed: confirm per lifecycle_rules.md (signed agreement + FSRU physically under construction → construction; onshore/mooring works start not yet confirmed). FSRU SYNC: vessel Hull 3407 has no name/IMO yet — cross-check the carrier project when named. Confidence: sponsor/location/agreement = high (Excelerate primary source); capacity/status = medium.

OtherNames: Buga LNG; Regasificadora del Pacifico; RDP
Location: FSU moored at Buenaventura (Pacific coast); regasification plant at Buga, Valle del Cauca (~connected by pipeline)
ResearcherNotesProject: Pacific-coast FSU-based import project (DISTINCT from GEM's existing Caribbean-coast 'Coveñas FSRU'). Owner Regasificadora del Pacifico (RDP, part of PIO SAS); Ecopetrol is offtaker/services counterparty (term contract Feb 2025); Exmar is FSU lessor/operator (5-yr firm charter secured Sept 2025). RDP reached financial close ~Feb 2026 ($130M senior debt, BTG Pactual + FDN). Operations expected Q3 2026. Split geography: FSU/marine at Buenaventura + regas plant at Buga (Valle del Cauca). CAPACITY: source gives 60 MMcf/d gas flow (~0.46 MTPA, DERIVED — not a source-stated MTPA); confirm. FSU vessel NOT yet named in any source — left blank (do not infer). entity_additions: 'Regasificadora del Pacifico' and 'PIO SAS' are new (not in GEM local entity data) — confirm against live entity system before creating. Confidence: sponsor/financing/status = high; capacity = medium (derived).
